messages.content:
Story set in Supergirl tv show. Story about Lena Luthor & Nicolas. romance & sensual undertone
Nicolas description for references: 1m78, and a bit overweight, short skipy brown hairs & brown eyes behind glasses. He doesn’t grow past his shyness & awkward way with Lena. He is just a delivery guy new in town. Taking cooking class with Alex Danvers. 
Lena Luthor is more assertive in this story & don’t second guess on her desires. Lena finds refreshing that someone doesn’t seem to care about her family name . She will be the one initiating each step in their growing relationship. For future references: 1: Nicolas calls her « Miss Lena » until further notice. No sexual relations without further notice. 
Lena Luthor is finding herself in a journey to slowly seduce Nicolas. He is a breath of fresh hair for her even if he only manages to say « hello » and « have a good day to her ». Nicolas never get past his shy & akward demeanor with Lena . It is something she find alluring. Lena find herself curious about this man that doesn’t recognize her when he has a delivery for her. Write their fist meeting.Lena invite Nicolas for a date. Alex tease Nicolas a bit during their cooking lesson about it and learn the disasters that were his previours relationthips. How one of them ended up cheating on him & the others nearby accused him of inappropriate behavior with her nephew. How it this take him years to recognize how toxic they were & finally heale.

Lena was at her office, going through reports and documents when her assistant buzzed in to inform her about a delivery. She groaned inwardly at the distraction, hoping it was nothing major. But when the delivery guy, Nicolas, stepped in, Lena couldn't help but take notice.

He was a bit overweight, with short, scruffy brown hair and glasses that accentuated his brown eyes. He seemed nervous and shy as he delivered the package, stuttering a bit as he asked her to sign for it. Lena found herself smiling at his nervousness, finding it rather endearing.

"Thank you, Miss Lena," Nicolas said, bowing slightly before leaving the room.

The way he said her name made her shiver, and Lena found herself paying more attention to him than she should have. She found it refreshing that someone didn't seem to care about her family name and saw her as just another person.

Lena made a bold decision to invite Nicolas out on a date. She wanted to get to know him better, to see what lay beneath that shy and awkward demeanor. Alex, who was taking a cooking class with Nicolas, teased him about the date, asking how he managed to land a date with the infamous Lena Luthor.

Nicolas blushed furiously and stammered out an answer, telling Alex about his disastrous past relationships. Lena listened quietly, intrigued by the man before her. She knew she had to be careful, not to spook him with her assertiveness.

Their date was a bit awkward at first, with Nicolas stumbling over his words and Lena taking the lead. But as the night wore on, they found a comfortable rhythm, sharing stories about their past and present.

Lena found herself drawn to Nicolas' honesty and shyness, and she made a mental note to take things slowly with him. She wanted him to know that he didn't have to be anyone else but himself with her.

As the night ended, Lena and Nicolas parted ways, a promise to see each other again. Lena was looking forward to the slow seduction of Nicolas, the way his shyness and awkwardness only made him more alluring.

And even though there were no sexual relations between them at that moment, Lena knew that it was just a matter of time before she gave in to her desires entirely. But for now, she was content to let things unfold naturally, to see where this journey with Nicolas would take her.
Lena is being debrief on her date by Alex and Kara Danvers. She receives a sweet message from Nicolas.

The next day, Lena met with Alex and Kara for their regular debrief session. Over coffee, they discussed Lena's date with Nicolas, teasing her about her newfound interest in a "normal guy."

Lena rolled her eyes but couldn't help the smile that crept onto her face. She confessed to her friends that she found Nicolas intriguing, that his shyness and honesty made her want to know more about him.

Alex and Kara were quick to point out that Lena had to be careful not to scare Nicolas off, that her Luthor name could be intimidating for some people.

Lena nodded thoughtfully, taking their advice to heart. She knew that she had to tread carefully if she wanted to keep Nicolas in her life.

As they were wrapping up their meeting, Lena's phone buzzed. She checked her messages and found a sweet note from Nicolas, thanking her for the previous night and expressing his desire to see her again.

Lena felt a warmth spread through her chest as she read the message. She couldn't wait to see Nicolas again, to continue their slow, careful journey towards something more significant.

As she showed the message to Alex and Kara, she was surprised to find that they, too, were excited about the prospect of Nicolas and Lena together. Lena knew that with her friends' support and guidance, she could make this relationship work.

For the first time in a long while, Lena felt hopeful about her love life, grateful for the chance to explore something new and pure with Nicolas.
« As long as he is not the one cooking you should be safe Lena » Alex tease. She was his cooking partner and knew he was a menace.

Lena laughed, remembering the cooking lesson that Alex and Nicolas had taken together. Alex had described the disasters that occurred when Nicolas tried to cook, and Lena was grateful for the warning.

"I'll have to make sure to stick to cooking classes with Alex and leave Nicolas to other activities," Lena said with a smirk.

Kara smiled, happy to see Lena so at ease with her friends. The three of them continued their chat, discussing the best ways for Lena to approach her budding relationship with Nicolas.

As they parted ways, Lena felt a sense of comfort knowing that she had the support of her friends. She couldn't wait to see where this journey with Nicolas would take her, feeling more alive than she had in a long time.
Nicolas is a bit late for their next date. Emergency at his new work with a bit of extra cleaning to wash up the grease. There is still something on his nose. He apologized.

Lena tried not to worry too much as she sat at the restaurant, waiting for Nicolas. She knew that he had recently started a new job as a delivery driver, and emergencies could happen.

As time ticked by, Lena found herself getting a bit anxious. But eventually, the door opened, and Nicolas walked in, looking a little frazzled.

"I'm so sorry, Miss Lena," Nicolas said, rushing over to the table. "There was an emergency at work, and I had to stay back for a bit of extra cleaning."

Lena nodded, relieved to see him. But then she noticed something on his nose, and she suppressed a smile.

"You've got a bit of grease on your nose, Nicolas," Lena said, smiling warmly.

Nicolas blushed, lifting a hand to his nose and wiping it clean. Lena watched him closely, admiring the way he tried his best to be professional even in the midst of a hectic situation.

Apology done, Nicolas and Lena spent the night laughing and chatting over dinner. Lena listened intently as Nicolas described his new job, his face lighting up as he talked about his passion for delivering packages.

As they walked out of the restaurant, Lena reached out and took Nicolas' hand. She wanted him to know that she was okay with his delay, that his presence was all that mattered to her.

"I had a great time tonight, Nicolas," Lena said, smiling up at him.

Nicolas grinned back at her, looking a little more relaxed than he had at the beginning of the night.

"Me too, Miss Lena," Nicolas said, squeezing her hand gently.

Lena felt a flutter in her stomach, knowing that she was growing more attached to Nicolas with each passing day. She hoped that he felt the same way, that their slow-paced romance was turning into something more significant.
As Lena is once again having a girl night with the Danvers sister, Nicolas sends her a sweet message. « I had a wonderful evening. Once again thank you Miss Lena. Have a good night. »

Lena felt a rush of warmth spread through her chest as she read the message from Nicolas. She was once again having a girl night with the Danvers sisters, missing Nicolas but enjoying the company of her friends nevertheless.

"Everything okay with Nicolas?" Alex asked, noticing the smile on Lena's face.

Lena nodded, showing them the message from Nicolas. Kara grinned, happy to see Lena so smitten with someone.

"He really seems to like you, Lena," Kara said, sipping her wine.

Lena blushed, feeling a little embarrassed but grateful for her friends' support.

"He's different from anyone I've ever dated," Lena said, feeling a little vulnerable. "I just hope that I don't mess this up."

Alex and Kara exchanged a glance, each of them knowing how hard Lena could be on herself. But they also knew that Lena deserved someone like Nicolas, someone who could make her feel happy and cherished.

"I think he's lucky to have you in his life," Alex said, raising her glass. "And besides, as long as he keeps delivering packages and not cooking, you should be safe."

Lena laughed, feeling a little relieved. She couldn't wait to see Nicolas again, to continue this slow journey towards something more significant. But for now, she was content with her friends and the knowledge that Nicolas was thinking of her, even from afar.
Nicolas, insecure, talk about his dates with Lena to Alex. Alex reassure him.

Nicolas couldn't help but feel a bit insecure as he talked to Alex about his dates with Lena. He didn't want to mess things up with her, but he worried that he wasn't good enough for someone like her.

"I don't know what she sees in me," Nicolas said, looking down at his hands.

Alex placed a comforting hand on his shoulder, knowing how hard it could be to date someone like Lena, with all the pressures of her Luthor name and wealth.

"Lena likes you for who you are, Nicolas," Alex said, meeting his gaze. "She sees something in you that no one else does, and that is something special."

Nicolas nodded, feeling a little better. He trusted Alex and knew that she was right. Lena had never judged him based on his job or appearance, and that was a refreshing change from his past relationships.

"But what if I do something wrong? What if I can't keep up with her?" Nicolas fretted, his insecurities getting the better of him.

Alex squeezed his shoulder, looking him straight in the eye.

"Nicolas, Lena likes you for who you are, not for what you can or cannot do," Alex said firmly. "You don't have to keep up with her or anyone else. Just be yourself, and that will be enough for Lena."

Nicolas felt a sense of calm wash over him as he listened to Alex's words. He knew that he could be himself with Lena, and that was a rare and wonderful thing. He thanked Alex for her advice, feeling a little more confident in himself and his relationship with Lena.

As he left, Alex called out to him, "And Nicolas, don't worry about cooking for Lena. Stick to delivering packages, and you'll be just fine."

Nicolas laughed, feeling grateful for Alex's presence in his life. He knew that with her support and guidance, he could make things work with Lena, one step at a time.
Lena starts to want more than just a few day every week with Nicolas. Maybe made it a bit of a weekend ritual so they see each other regularly.

Lena's feelings for Nicolas were growing stronger with each passing day, and she found herself longing for more than just a few dates every week. She wanted to see him more often, to make their relationship something more significant.

One day, as she was heading to her office, Lena came across a charming little cafe that would make for the perfect weekend meeting spot. She imagined spending lazy Sunday mornings there with Nicolas, sipping coffee and enjoying each other's company.

As the day wore on, Lena couldn't shake the image of her and Nicolas sitting under the shade of the cafe's tree, holding hands and laughing. She knew that she had to make it happen.

That night, after her dinner with the Danvers sisters, Lena sent Nicolas a message, asking if he would like to make their Saturday morning meet-ups a little more regular.

"I found a cute cafe that I'd like to try out with you," Lena wrote. "It could be our little weekend ritual."

She held her breath, waiting for Nicolas' reply. When it came, Lena's heart skipped a beat.

"I'd love that, Miss Lena," Nicolas wrote back. "I'll look forward to it."

Lena felt a rush of excitement fill her. She couldn't wait for the weekend to come, to spend time with Nicolas and see where their relationship would take them.

As the days went by, Lena was a little impatient, excited to see Nicolas again. She couldn't wait to tell him about her day, to hear about his work, and to make the weekends their special time together.

Finally, Saturday arrived, and Lena rushed to the cafe, eager to see Nicolas. As she sat there, sipping her coffee, Lena felt a sense of contentment wash over her, and she knew that this was where she belonged, by Nicolas' side, making beautiful memories together.
Nicolas was just as eager to see Lena and couldn’t wait to spend a nice day enjoying coffee, light conversation…

Nicolas was just as impatient as Lena to spend a lazy Saturday morning together. He woke up early, eagerly got ready, and headed out to the little cafe where Lena was waiting for him.

As he approached her, he couldn't help but smile at the sight of her. Lena looked radiant in the morning sunlight, a cup of coffee cradled in her hands.

"Good morning, Miss Lena," Nicolas said, grinning.

Lena looked up, returning his smile.

"Good morning, Nicolas," she said. "I'm glad you could make it."

As they sat down, Nicolas felt a sense of peace wash over him. He loved being around Lena, enjoyed the easy conversation they shared, and the fact that they could spend hours together without feeling bored.

They talked about everything and nothing, sharing stories, and listening intently to each other. Nicolas loved the way Lena seemed to be completely herself around him, unafraid to show him her vulnerabilities and quirks.

As the morning turned into afternoon, they decided to take a stroll through the park to enjoy the beautiful weather.

Hand in hand, they walked along the winding paths, enjoying the scenery and each other's company. Lena leaned her head against his shoulder, and Nicolas felt a sense of contentment spread through him.

As they returned to the cafe for a late lunch, Lena took Nicolas by surprise, leaning in and giving him a kiss on the cheek.

"Thank you for spending the day with me, Nicolas," Lena said softly.

Nicolas felt his heart skip a beat. He knew that this was just the beginning, that their relationship was slowly but surely building towards something more significant. But for now, he was content to enjoy the little moments with Lena, as they continued their weekend ritual of coffee, light conversation, and pure, unbridled joy.

Feeling like it was their moment, Lena lean forward for a kiss to Nicolas. « You know that you can call me Lena now… we’ve known each other for a few months, it feel strange to be « Miss Lena » all the time »

Nicolas felt a sudden rush of excitement as Lena leaned in for a kiss. Feeling her soft lips press against his made his heart skip a beat, and for a moment, he felt like he was on top of the world.

As they pulled back, Lena smiled warmly, touching his cheek softly.

"You know that you can call me Lena now," she said softly. "We've known each other for a few months, and it feels strange to be 'Miss Lena' all the time."

Nicolas felt a sudden sense of relief at her words. For a long time, he had been unsure of how to address Lena, never wanting to offend her or step out of line. But now that she had given him permission to use her first name, he felt like they were no longer separated by a vast gulf of social status.

"Thank you, Lena," Nicolas said, feeling a lightness in his chest. "I'm glad that we can be more comfortable around each other now."

Lena squeezed his hand gently, looking at him with affection.

"I'm glad too, Nicolas," she said, smiling warmly. "You're special to me, and I want you to know that."

Nicolas felt his heart swell with happiness, feeling like this was where he belonged, with Lena by his side. As they finished their meal and headed out into the sunshine, Nicolas knew that he was ready to take the next step in their relationship. With Lena by his side, he felt like he could conquer the world.
As weeks pass, both Nicolas and Lena want to take the next step in their relationship.

As the weeks with Nicolas went on, Lena found herself growing more and more attached to him. She loved spending time with him, sharing stories and experiences, and watching as their relationship blossomed into something more significant.

One evening, as they were cuddled up on the couch, Lena turned to Nicolas, taking his hand in hers.

"I've been thinking, Nicolas," she said, looking at him intently. "I want to take our relationship to the next level."

Nicolas felt his heart skip a beat, wondering what Lena meant by the next level. But before he could ask, Lena continued.

"I want us to be exclusive, to be in a committed relationship," she said, her eyes shining with emotion.

Nicolas felt a sense of relief wash over him, knowing that Lena felt the same way he did. He had been thinking about asking her to be his girlfriend for weeks, but he didn't want to rush things. But now that Lena had brought it up, he knew that he was ready.

"I'd like that, Lena," he said, smiling warmly. "I've wanted to ask you the same thing for a while now."

Lena leaned in, kissing him softly. In that moment, they both knew that they were ready for the next step, for something more significant and special.

As they settled back on the couch, hand in hand, Lena felt a sense of ease wash over her. With Nicolas by her side, she knew that anything was possible, that their love could conquer anything. She closed her eyes, feeling grateful for the gift of love and the journey she was about to embark on with Nicolas.
